pi_items.metadata_value: CRA Guide T4037 (Capital Gains) 2024 and CRA Interpretation Bulletin IT-387R2 (Superficial Loss)
pi_items.verified_value: CRA Guide T4037 (Capital Gains) 2024 and CRA Interpretation Bulletin IT-387R2 (Superficial Loss)
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed T4037 is the real CRA guide for Capital Gains and IT-387R2 is the Interpretation Bulletin on Identical Properties encompassing superficial losses. The synthetic scenario is modeled on these rules.
Explicitly stated in scenario_nair_crypto.txt under 'PERSONAL INFORMATION'.
Explicitly stated in scenario_nair_crypto.txt as 'British Columbia (BC)'.

pi_items.metadata_value: Multiple Schedule 3 capital gain entries; superficial loss rule (ITA s.54); T5 staking rewards as interest; T777 home office; RRSP
pi_items.verified_value: Multiple Schedule 3 capital gain entries; superficial loss rule (ITA s.54); T5 staking rewards as interest; T777 home office; RRSP
pi_items.source: Confirmed directly in scenario_nair_crypto.txt which contains Schedule 3 entries (ETH, BTC, MATIC), an explicit ITA s.54 superficial loss for SOL, T5 staking ($1,840), T777 home office ($2,288), and RRSP ($5,500).
Confirmed via setup_task.ps1 file target paths and task.json description.
Confirmed by locating scenario_nair_crypto.txt in the gym environment's data directory.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated tax scenario data for Priya Nair, a British Columbia resident working as a software engineer at Shopify and investing in cryptocurrency. The data requires applying real CRA rules from Guide T4037 and Interpretation Bulletin IT-387R2. Verified complexities include multiple Schedule 3 capital gains (ETH, BTC, MATIC), a denied superficial loss for SOL under ITA s.54, $1,840 in T5 staking rewards treated as interest, $2,288 in T777 home office deductions, and a $5,500 RRSP contribution. The agent must save the return to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\StudioTax\priya_nair.24t'.
